title: I Love Sally Rooney’s Books but I Love Her Essay About Competitive College Debate Even More
description:
A typical conversation about Sally Rooney often includes some version of the question: Are you a Normal People person or Conversations with Friends person? Rooney readers tend to have a strong, if not fraught, preference. Whenever people have asked me this question, however, I’ve had a different answer. “Actually,” I say, “I’m an ‘Even if […]

The post I Love Sa
hook: Sally Rooney's essay on competitive debate reveals more about her brilliance than her celebrated novels ever could.

title: I Will Survive
description: The “Survivor” fandom becomes more obsessive with every passing season.
hook: How Survivor fandom became increasingly obsessive—a deep dive into reality TV's hold on cultural imagination.

title: Years ago, novelist Tayari Jones snuck into a writing class. It changed her life
description: Jones' new novel, Kin, is set in 1950s Louisiana and Atlanta, and tells the story of two young women who grow up next door to each other without their mothers.
hook: Tayari Jones's late-bloomer origin story: sneaking into a writing class that transformed her into a celebrated novelist.

title: Lords of the Ring
hook: The cultural politics of sumo wrestling—high art meets athletic tradition in unexpected ways.

title: Nonprofessional Actors Are the Heart of the Movies
description: This year’s leading Oscar contenders are invigorated by performers notable for their personalities and wider-world accomplishments.
hook: Nonprofessional actors are reshaping prestige cinema, bringing real-world magnetism to Oscar contenders.
